original: Batman debuts in the 27th issue of Detective Comics by DC Comics
At 15 years, 9 months American swimmerMichael Phelpsbreaks 200m butterfly world record at the US World Championship trials at Austin, Texas; becomes youngest male to set a world mark
Joe Ruby, American animator (co-creator of Scooby-Doo), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2020)
World's most valuable tip - New York police detective Robert Cunningham offers waitress Phyllis Penzo half of a $1 lottery ticket; the next day, they win $6 million
The United States buys Alaska from Russia for $7,200,000 (109 million in 2018 dollars), roughly 2 cents an acre

Key figures in Artificial Intelligence includingElon MuskandSteve Wozniaksign an open letter warning the race to develop AI systems is out of control and asking for a suspension of at least six months
Marcos Pontes is the first Brazilian astronaut in space
World War II: a defecting German pilot delivers a Messerschmitt Me 262A-1 to Americans
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 169,600' (51,690 m)
First recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
MC Hammer [Stanley Kirk Burrell], American rapper (Hammer Time), born in Oakland, California
3rd NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Columbia 3 lands at White Sands, New Mexico
Dalai Lamaflees China and is granted political asylum in India
Ether used as an anaesthetic for 1st time by Dr Crawford Long (Georgia)
PBA National Championship Won by Mike Miller
International Olympic Committee announces postponed 2020 Summer Olympic Games will be held July 23-August 8 in 2021 because of the COVID-19 pandemic
Swedish gamer and YouTuberPewDiePieovertaken by Indian channel T-Series as YouTube channel with most subscribers (both on 92 million)
Pencil with attached eraser patented (Hyman L Lipman of Philadelphia)
Computer's Turing Award won by American programmer Jack Dongarra, whose work paved the way for supercomputers
Sergei Ilyushin, Russian airplane builder (designed Il-2 Shturmovik - world's most produced military aircraft), born in Dilyalevo, Russia (d. 1977)
Albert Einsteinannounces revised unified field theory
Anna Sewell, English author (Black Beauty), born in Great Yarmouth, Norfolk (d. 1878)
Cover picture of Beatles' "Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" album is photographed by Michael Cooper
Vincent van Gogh's "Sunflowers" sells for a record £22.5 million ($39.7 million)
Carl Friedrich Gauss, German mathematician, discovers the construction of the heptadecagon
Bell Telephone Laboratories announces invention of the phototransistor in Murray Hill, New Jersey
Bryan Molloy, Scottish chemist (co-inventor of Prozac), born in Dundee, Scotland (d. 2004)[1]
Richard Evans, British graphic designer, album cover designer (The Who; The Doors; Pink Floyd; Louis Armstrong), born in Wilmslow, Cheshire
Marc Davis, American animator (Walt Disney), born in Bakersfield, California (d. 2000)
Man accidentally backs into Oakland A's JoseCanseco's $225,000 Lamborghini
Simone Ashley [Ashwini Pillai], English-Indian actress (Bridgerton, Sex Education, F1), born in Camberley, England
Cha Eun-woo, South Korean singer (Astro), actor and model, born in Gunpo, South Korea
Anitta [Larissa de Macedo Machado], Brazilian pop and reggaeton singer-songwriter (Show das Poderosas; Envolver), born in Rio Janeiro, Brazil
Bill Johnson, American alpine skier (first American to win alpine Olympic gold - 1984), born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2016)
Test Cricket debut of West Indian greatGarfield Sobersvs England at Kingston Oval, Barbados
England Cricket all out for 46 at Port-of-Spain
Jack Nicklausdebuts on the Senior PGA Tour with a 71 (-1) in the first round of The Tradition at Desert Mountain and wins the event by four strokes overGary Player
"Chariots of Fire" directed by Hugh Hudson and starring Ben Cross and Ian Charleson premieres at a Royal Command Film Performance (Best Picture 1982)
British inventor Birt Acres films Oxford and Cambridge boat race
Newfoundland changes time to 3½ hrs W of Greenwich, repeats 44 sec
Lötschberg tunnel in Switzerland (13,735 m) completed
Max Tishler, American organic chemist at Merck who synthesized Vitamin B2 and developed antibiotic for poultry, born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1989)[1]
Stanley Cup Final, Patrick Arena, Victoria, BC: Victoria Cougars (WCHL) beat Montreal Canadiens (NHL), 6-1 for a 3-1 series win; last non-NHL team to win trophy
Stanley Cup, Mutual Street Arena, Toronto, ON: Toronto Arenas (NHL) beat Vancouver Millionaires (PCHA), 2-1 for a 3-2 series victory; first series contested by the new NHL
Stanley Cup Final, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Canadiens (NHA) beat Portland Rosebuds (PCHA), 2-1 for a 3-2 series victory
Northern Michigan wins its 1st NCAA hockey title
Punjab beat Maharashtra by 120 runs to win Cricket's Ranji Trophy
TV network Channel 5 begins broadcasting in the UK
Maurice LaMarche, Canadian-American comedian and voice actor (Hey Arnold!), born in Toronto, Ontario
Hayashibara Megumi, Japanese voice actress, singer (Give A Reason), and lyricist, born in Kita Tokyo, Japan
Graeme Edge, British rock drummer, and poet (Moody Blues - "The Dream"; "Morning Glory"), born in Rocester, Staffordshire England (d. 2021)
Hebe Tian, Taiwanese Mando-pop singer (S.H.E), born in Xinfeng, Taiwan
Samantha Stosur, Australian tennis player (US Open singles 2011; 9 x Grand Slam doubles titles; Tour Finals doubles 2005, 06), born in Brisbane, Australia
Ji Soo, South Korean actor, born in Busan, South Korea
Tao Geoghegan Hart, British road cyclist (Giro d'Italia 2020), born in London, England
Shervin Hajipour, Iranian singer-songwriter (Baraye), born in Babolsar, Iran
Thomas Rhett, American singer-songwriter, born in Valdosta, Tennessee
Matthew Pritchard, Welsh Stuntman and celebrity chef, born in Cardiff, Wales
Lene Lovich, English-American singer, songwriter and musician (Lucky Number), born in Detroit, Michigan
Sam Clayton, American percussionist, congueros, and vocalist (Little Feat, Little Richard, Jimmy Buffett), born in New Orleans, Louisiana
